heading: Always Carry Bags
body: A backpack dramatically increases your carry capacity. Find a large hiking bag or duffel bag early in your run. Organise bags by purpose: one for weapons and tools, one for food and water. Being overloaded reduces movement speed and increases stamina drain — travel light on combat runs and only take what you need.

heading: Secure a Vehicle Early
body: Cars are found in most car parks and driveways. A working vehicle is critical for looting runs, outrunning large hordes, and emergency extraction from dangerous situations. Keep a spare tyre and a full fuel can in the boot at all times. Learn basic vehicle maintenance to avoid being stranded. Do not block your own base exit with vehicles.

heading: Manage Noise and Smell
body: Gunshots attract zombies from a very large radius — use firearms as a last resort or as a planned distraction tactic, not for routine clearing. Sprinting through zombie groups raises your noise level. Blood on your clothing increases the chance of nearby zombies detecting you. Change or wash bloodied clothing after major encounters to reduce your scent trail.

heading: Build a Safe Sleep Location
body: Sleep restores fatigue and accelerates skill learning from books. Always fully secure your sleep location before resting: lock all doors, board windows, and clear the immediate surrounding area. Sleeping in a second-floor room with the stairs removed below is the safest approach. Set an in-game alarm to avoid oversleeping if you expect activity.

heading: Skill Books Multiply XP Gain
body: Reading a skill book before practising a skill multiplies your XP gain by 2x to 4x depending on the book level. Prioritise finding skill books for skills you actively use — carpentry, first aid, and cooking deliver the highest return in the early game. Skill books are found in libraries, schools, and on residential bookshelves.
